text.title: REST API Design Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in shipped api-rest-design work. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Path is /createOrder + POST; consumer has to read docs to know what HTTP method to use.
antipattern.root-cause: Author thought of HTTP as transport, not protocol.
antipattern.fix: Rename to /orders POST; redirect old verb-path to new for one major version. See rule plural-resource-paths.

antipattern.symptom: GET /charges/{id}/retry triggers a new charge attempt; retries duplicate-charge the customer.
antipattern.fix: Convert to POST /charges/{id}/retries with Idempotency-Key. See rule verb-idempotency-match.

antipattern.symptom: Every response returns 200-OK; clients parse body to know if it failed.
antipattern.root-cause: Author confused HTTP layer with application layer.
antipattern.fix: Map outcomes to RFC 9110 codes: 201/204/400/401/403/404/409/422/429/5xx. See rule status-codes-rfc-9110.

antipattern.symptom: Network retry on /charges creates two charges; customer complains; refund issued.
antipattern.fix: Accept Idempotency-Key header; cache response >= 24h. See rule idempotency-key-on-charge.
